Politics.headline: Senate Rejects Curb on Trump Military Action in Cuba
Politics.detail: The Senate rejected an effort to advance legislation that would bar U.S. military action against Cuba without Congress' green light. Republicans continue to stand behind Trump as he acts unilaterally to exert American force in a range of global conflicts, including Venezuela, Iran and Cuba.

Politics.headline: James Comey Indicted Again Over Social Media Post
Politics.detail: Former FBI Director James Comey has been indicted by the U.S. Department of Justice for a second time, this time over a 2025 social media post featuring seashells arranged to display "86 47," which some Republicans interpreted as a threat against Trump. It's the second time the Trump administration has attempted to prosecute him.

Politics.headline: King Charles praises Nato and urges defence of Ukraine in key speech during Trump visit
Politics.detail: During his visit to the US, King Charles addressed Congress, emphasizing the importance of NATO unity and continued support for Ukraine. He became just the second monarch to address Congress, delivering a message of unity.

Politics.headline: FCC Orders Review of ABC Licenses After Jimmy Kimmel’s Melania Trump Joke
Politics.detail: The FCC is ramping up the pressure on ABC and Disney by threatening to strip broadcasters of their station licenses. The FCC says the review of the licenses is tied to Disney's DEI initiatives, but it's widely seen as retaliation for a joke made by Jimmy Kimmel about Melania Trump.

Politics.headline: Higher oil prices set to give feds a 'windfall' in next budget
Politics.detail: Global energy markets continue to reel from the ongoing disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to higher oil prices. The price of oil is sitting around $100 US a barrel, but Tuesday's spring economic update used much lower prices to build the budget.

Finance & Economy.headline: Kairos Power's bet on nuclear and the AI data center sector
Finance & Economy.detail: An article discusses Kairos Power's investment in nuclear energy and its potential impact on the AI data center sector.

Finance & Economy.headline: Markets spike, oil drops sharply on opening of Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: Financial markets react positively while oil prices decrease significantly following the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Finance & Economy.headline: UAE pulls out of OPEC, effective May 1, shaking up global oil market.
Finance & Economy.detail: The United Arab Emirates' decision to withdraw from OPEC is set to take effect on May 1st, which is causing disruption in the global oil market.

Finance & Economy.headline: Nearly half of Gen X workers are delaying retirement as rising costs, stagnant wages drain savings.
Finance & Economy.detail: A new survey reveals that nearly 50% of Gen X workers are postponing their retirement plans because of increased living expenses and stagnant income.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil plunges below $95 as the Dow surges 1,100 in a worldwide rally following a ceasefire with Iran.
Finance & Economy.detail: With a tentative ceasefire in place with Iran, oil prices are plummeting and the Dow Jones is experiencing a global surge.

Technology.headline: US restricts equipment exports to Hua Hong, China's No. 2 chipmaker
Technology.detail: The U. S. Department of Commerce has ordered several chip equipment companies to stop shipping certain tools to Hua Hong, China's second-largest chipmaker, aiming to slow China's progress in developing advanced chips. The restrictions apply to facilities believed to manufacture some of China's most sophisticated chips and may heighten tensions with China.

Technology.headline: Tieto Tech Consulting's transformation progressing in demanding market environment – new efficiency measures initiated
Technology.detail: Tieto Tech Consulting is resetting its operating model to strengthen accountability and enhance customer focus, integrating subsidiaries and driving an AI-focused competence shift.  The company is planning to reduce a maximum of 500 employees across geographies due to challenging market conditions.

Technology.headline: Commission urges Member States to rollout EU age verification app
Technology.detail: The European Commission is urging member states to accelerate the rollout of the EU age verification app, aiming to protect children from harmful online content. The app allows users to prove they meet age requirements without revealing personal details and can be integrated into a European Digital Identity Wallet.

Technology.headline: GoTo Expands Little Caesars Technology Deployment
Technology.detail: GoTo, a cloud communications and IT company, is expanding its technology deployment with Little Caesars to improve consistency and visibility across locations, making it easier for restaurants to onboard and serve guests. GoTo's footprint will expand through Summer 2026 to include additional GoTo Connect deployments at franchise restaurants and select Ilitch Companies' venues.

Technology.headline: Blacksburg micro-3D printing developer gets patent, Virginia Tech team hunts for microplastics on fish scales
Technology.detail: Skyphos Technologies, a Blacksburg company specializing in micro-3D printing, received a U.S. patent for a 3D printing process that could significantly accelerate the manufacturing of tiny, high-precision devices. A Virginia Tech-led team is also using high tech to hunt for microplastics on fish scales, addressing the challenge of detecting microplastics in seafood.

Health.headline: Sleep Disorders Very Common in Patients With Osteoarthritis
Health.detail: A recent study highlights the prevalence of sleep disorders among individuals suffering from osteoarthritis, suggesting a potential link between the two conditions.

Health.headline: Museum Artworks Offer Lessons in Clinical Vision
Health.detail: Medscape Medical News discusses how analyzing museum artworks can provide valuable insights and training for professionals in the field of clinical vision.

Health.headline: MIT Scientists Turn Chaotic Laser Light Into Powerful Brain Imaging Tool
Health.detail: Scientists at MIT discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused beam, enabling them to image the blood-brain barrier in 3D at faster speeds.

Health.headline: Vitamin D Boosts Breast Cancer Treatment Success by 79%
Health.detail: A small study found that women taking daily low doses of vitamin D alongside chemotherapy were significantly more likely to see their breast cancer vanish compared to those who didn't.

Health.headline: Glioblastoma mapping uncovers four recurring tumor cell communities, revealing treatment targets.
Health.detail: Researchers have identified four recurring tumor cell communities in glioblastoma, which could lead to the development of new treatment targets for this aggressive brain cancer.

Sports.headline: Bobby Witt Jr. hits 3-run homer in 10th to lift Royals over Athletics 4-1 for 4th straight win
Sports.detail: Bobby Witt Jr.'s 3-run homer in the 10th inning propelled the Royals to a 4-1 victory over the Athletics, marking their fourth consecutive win.

Sports.headline: Jalen Brunson scores 39 points and the Knicks rout the Hawks 126-97 for a 3-2 series lead
Sports.detail: Jalen Brunson's impressive 39-point performance led the Knicks to a dominant 126-97 victory over the Hawks, giving them a 3-2 lead in the series.

Sports.headline: England, Chelsea defender Millie Bright retires from soccer because of injuries
Sports.detail: Millie Bright, the defender for England and Chelsea, has announced her retirement from soccer due to injuries.

Sports.headline: PSG edge out Bayern in record nine-goal semi-final first leg
Sports.detail: Paris St-Germain beat Bayern Munich 5-4 in a record-breaking Champions League semi-final first leg.

Sports.headline: Babar Azam matches record held by Virat Kohli, Abhishek Sharma with blistering 2nd century in 4 PSL matches
Sports.detail: Babar Azam has equaled a record held by Virat Kohli and Abhishek Sharma by scoring his second century in just 4 PSL matches.

Entertainment.headline: He-Man creator Roger Sweet dies at 91
Entertainment.detail: Roger Sweet, the creator of the He-Man character and action figure, passed away at the age of 91 after battling dementia. Sweet was instrumental in developing the prototype for He-Man during his time at Mattel in the '70s and '80s.

Entertainment.headline: Beyoncé's back and 'Fashion Is Art': What to know about the 2026 Met Gala
Entertainment.detail: Beyoncé will co-chair the Met Gala alongside Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams, and Anna Wintour, marking her return after a decade-long absence. This year's theme is “Costume Art” with a dress code of “Fashion Is Art,” and the event is moving to the museum's new Condé M. Nast Galleries.

Entertainment.headline: Laura Dern Joins ‘White Lotus’ Season 4
Entertainment.detail: Laura Dern has joined the cast of 'White Lotus' Season 4, replacing Helena Bonham Carter. The details of her role are currently unknown.

Entertainment.headline: Ariana Grande Announces 'Petal' Album
Entertainment.detail: Ariana Grande has announced her new album, 'Petal,' which is set to be released later this year. Further details, including the release date and tracklist, are forthcoming.

Entertainment.headline: Tupac's stepbrother files wrongful death suit, cites new turns in 29-year-old murder case
Entertainment.detail: Tupac Shakur's stepbrother has filed a wrongful death lawsuit, citing new developments in the 29-year-old murder case. The suit comes amidst renewed interest and investigation into the unsolved killing of the rap icon.

Crime & Justice.headline: Europol task force nets 280 arrests as 'violence for hire' spreads across Europe
Crime & Justice.detail: European Union police agency Europol says that an international task force formed to crack down on violence as a criminal service has led to 280 arrests in its first year.

Crime & Justice.headline: Call centres dismantled and ten arrested in EUR 50 million online fraud case
Crime & Justice.detail: A criminal network operating a large-scale online fraud scheme has been dismantled through a collaborative investigation involving Austrian and Albanian authorities, with support from Europol and Eurojust, leading to ten arrests and the seizure of nearly EUR 900,000 in cash. The call centers, located in Tirana, Albania, allegedly caused at least EUR 50 million in financial damage.

Crime & Justice.headline: Greek pensioner, 89, arrested following shooting rampage
Crime & Justice.detail: An 89-year-old Greek pensioner was arrested after a shooting rampage, though the gunman's motive remains unclear.

Crime & Justice.headline: Gunmen kill at least 29 in Nigeria's northeast Adamawa State
Crime & Justice.detail: Gunmen killed at least 29 people in Nigeria's northeast Adamawa State.

Crime & Justice.headline: WHCA DINNER SHOOTING.
Crime & Justice.detail: News about the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner.

Lifestyle.headline: 'An infection had started': Huda Beauty founder opens up about ruptured breast implant, urges fans to get them 'replaced every 10 years'
Lifestyle.detail: Huda Kattan, the founder of Huda Beauty, shared her experience with a ruptured breast implant and emphasized the importance of replacing them every 10 years to avoid complications. She is urging her fans to get them replaced every 10 years.

Lifestyle.headline: The wooden comb secret: Science behind the viral Japanese scalp ritual
Lifestyle.detail: A viral Japanese scalp ritual using wooden combs is gaining popularity, with science backing its benefits for scalp health. This ritual may help to keep your scalp fresh and healthy.

Lifestyle.headline: All the right moves! 17 personal trainers on the exercise they always recommend – from planks to face pulls
Lifestyle.detail: Seventeen personal trainers share their go-to exercises, ranging from planks to face pulls, offering a variety of options for improving fitness. These exercises can help you stay in shape.

Lifestyle.headline: Singer-actress Charlene Choi announces marriage to fitness instructor Elvis Lam
Lifestyle.detail: Cantopop star Charlene Choi, 43, announced her marriage to fitness instructor Elvis Lam on Instagram, expressing her joy in the news. She wrote, “Congratulations to me for marrying you. Congratulations to you for marrying me.”.

Lifestyle.headline: The 9 hottest sneaker collaborations of 2026: Nike, Adidas, Asics, Onitsuka Tiger and more
Lifestyle.detail: Fashion and sports brands are again creating aesthetic footwear combinations in 2026; here are some exciting collaborations from brands like Nike, Adidas, Asics and Onitsuka Tiger. These collaborations bring fashion and sports together.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion | A ingenious way to sidestep the dismal Senate filibuster
Opinion/Editorial.detail: George F. Will proposes a reform to the Senate filibuster, suggesting voters should ask Senate candidates if they support the reform and reject those who don't, to encourage a more functional Congress. He argues this would restrain the unconstrained presidency and promote checks and balances.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Keep AI out of Tisch
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Alice Rogers argues against NYU's expanding use of generative AI tools in its Tisch School of the Arts, emphasizing the importance of human storytelling and warning against the dangers of unchecked AI tools from corporations with questionable ethics. She suggests that the university's investment should be in human artistry, not in corporate interests that could exploit students.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Security should not be political
Opinion/Editorial.detail: TribLIVE.com argues against the politicization of security measures for elected officials, referencing a situation where the state treasurer refused to approve payments for security upgrades at the governor's private residence. The editorial emphasizes that combating political violence should be nonpartisan, and allowing it to become a political tug-of-war only feeds the enmity.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: CT's next generation isn't waiting. They're leading.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This CT Mirror opinion piece discusses the active role and leadership of young people in Connecticut's civic and political spheres, particularly in areas like gun violence prevention and electoral campaigns. The author highlights the energy and passion that young people bring to these endeavors, asserting that they are leaders today, not just of the future.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Pa. must properly fund new early literacy programs
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette argues that Pennsylvania must adequately fund its new early literacy programs to ensure their effectiveness, especially in struggling schools. The editorial stresses the importance of trained literacy coaches to support teachers and implement evidence-based curricula, advocating for sufficient resources to make a significant impact on childhood literacy.

World.headline: UAE exits OPEC
World.detail: The United Arab Emirates announced it is pulling out of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, citing rising global demand for oil and the ongoing conflict over the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE says it will exit the group on May 1st.

World.headline: King Charles III meets Trump for U.S. state visit amid British tensions
World.detail: King Charles III is traveling to New York City where he'll pay respects at the One World Trade Center memorial and take part in community events. He and his wife, Queen Camilla, were the guests of honor for a state visit at the White House where they were welcomed by President Trump.

World.headline: Trump threatens Iran to 'better get smart soon' amid stalled talks
World.detail: Amid stalled talks, Trump is warning Iran to 'better get smart soon'. The U.S. is weighing an Iranian proposal that would open the Strait of Hormuz but delay nuclear talks.

World.headline: Former FBI Director James Comey indicted again
World.detail: Former FBI Director James Comey is facing federal charges for the second time in seven months. Trump has long called for charges against Comey, asserting that he is guilty of crimes.

World.headline: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon kill at least seven
World.detail: At least seven people were killed in Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon on Tuesday, including three Lebanese Civil Defense rescuers. The ministry's Emergency Operations Center said the preliminary toll from the Israeli airstrike targeting the village of Majdal Zoun included five dead, among them three civil defense paramedics who were trapped under the rubble after being targeted while carrying out a rescue mission.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Warns Citizens in South Africa as Anti-Foreigner Protests Escalate
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean Embassy in South Africa has issued an urgent advisory to its nationals, urging them to stay alert, avoid protest areas, and remain indoors due to escalating anti-foreigner protests that threaten their safety and well-being. The embassy is closely monitoring the situation and engaging with South African authorities, advising citizens to report any threats to the South African Police Service and Zimbabwe's diplomatic offices.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ Employee Steals US$1 500, Blows It on Online Betting Aviator “Chindege”
Zimbabwe.detail: An employee of the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) allegedly stole US$1,500 and used it all on the online betting game Aviator, also known as “Chindege”. The iHarare news did not provide further details on the employee or the repercussions of this incident.

Zimbabwe.headline: Japan extends US$2,6 million agricultural equipment facility to Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: The Japanese government has extended a US$2,6 million facility to Zimbabwe for the procurement of agricultural equipment, aiming to boost the country's agricultural sector. This initiative is expected to enhance agricultural productivity and support local farmers.

Zimbabwe.headline: Fire brigade recues new Harare City Council sewer pipes from fire
Zimbabwe.detail: The Harare Fire Brigade successfully extinguished a fire that threatened new sewer pipes belonging to the Harare City Council. Quick response from the fire brigade prevented potential damage to the infrastructure.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chatunga Mugabe, co-accused expected in SA court for sentencing
Zimbabwe.detail: Chatunga Mugabe and his co-accused are expected to appear in a South African court for sentencing. Further details regarding the charges and potential penalties were not provided in the search results.
